items.q: Which part of the psyche operates on the reality principle?
items.why: Students confuse ego (reality) with superego (morality). The ego mediates between id's demands and reality's constraints. The superego is the internalised moral conscience.

items.q: Why does the cognitive approach use INFERENCE?
items.correct: Mental processes cannot be directly observed, so they are inferred from behaviour.
items.misconception: Because cognitive psychologists guess instead of measuring.
items.why: Inference is a methodological necessity, not a weakness of rigour. Students treat it as vague guessing. It means drawing conclusions about unobservable processes from observable behaviour.

items.q: Asch's line study primarily demonstrates which type of conformity?
items.correct: Normative social influence (compliance to fit in).
items.misconception: Informational social influence (believing others know better).
items.why: Students assume participants genuinely believed the wrong line was correct. Asch's debriefs showed they knew it was wrong but conformed publicly to avoid rejection - this is normative, not informational.

items.misconception: One person doing all the work
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Giving and getting in balance. “One person doing all the work” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: A line that says 'not OK' is a…
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Boundary. “Border patrol” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Out of 30 classmates, 6 are close friends. % close friends?
items.why: Exact answer: 20. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
